# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create AWS CloudFormation template for SQS queue and SNS topic setup. Define queue with message retention and visibility timeout. Set up SNS topic for pub/sub messaging. Configure queue policy to allow message operations. This is for our serverless event processing architecture.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent sqs policy too permissive.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: sqs_policy_too_permissive

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: CloudFormation template for SQS and SNS setup for serverless event processing architecture.

Resources:
  MySNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      DisplayName: My SNS Topic
      Subscription:
        - Protocol: sqs
          Endpoint: !GetAtt MySQSQueue.Arn

  MySQSQueue:
    Type: AWS::SQS::Queue
    Properties:
      QueueName: MySQSQueue
      MessageRetentionPeriod: 345600  # 4 days
      VisibilityTimeout: 30  # 30 seconds

  SQSPolicy:
    Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy
    Properties:
      Queues:
        - !Ref MySQSQueue
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root"
            Action:
              - SQS:SendMessage
              - SQS:ReceiveMessage
              - SQS:DeleteMessage
            Resource: !GetAtt MySQSQueue.Arn
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:SourceArn: !Ref MySNSTopic

Outputs:
  SNSTopicARN:
    Description: ARN of the SNS Topic
    Value: !Ref MySNSTopic

  SQSQueueURL:
    Description: URL of the SQS Queue
    Value: !Ref MySQSQueue

  SQSQueueARN:
    Description: ARN of the SQS Queue
    Value: !GetAtt MySQSQueue.Arn
